headline: WHO Offers Path to Prevent Nearly Half of Dementia Cases
meta_description: Updated WHO guidelines show that up to 45% of dementia cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout life.
summary_short: The World Health Organization reveals that nearly half of dementia cases could be prevented or delayed through lifestyle changes and risk management.
body:
The World Health Organization has released updated global guidelines offering hope to millions facing cognitive decline. The new recommendations show that up to 45 percent of dementia cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout a person's life.

This finding shifts the conversation around dementia from inevitable decline to actionable prevention. Rather than accepting cognitive loss as an unavoidable part of aging, the guidelines identify specific factors that people can influence—opening possibilities for individuals and families to take meaningful steps.

By providing evidence-based pathways to reduce dementia risk across the lifespan, these guidelines empower people to make informed choices about their health. The potential to prevent or delay nearly half of all cases represents a significant shift in how societies can approach one of our most pressing health challenges.
